name: Drunken Beans
recipeIngredient: 2 cup Dried pinto beans-picked over and soaked in cold water for at least 4 hours up to 12 hours. Drain.
5 1/2 quart Water plus additional if necessary
12 oz Dark Mexican beer, such as Negra Modelo or Dos Equis
2 tsp Salt - plus 1/2 more if necessary
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 300°F.
2. In a 6- to 7-quart ovenproof kettle, cook bacon over moderately high heat until browned. Stir and scrape up any brown bits that form.
3. Add onion and cook until lightened in color, about 5 minutes.
4. Add oregano and garlic, stirring, for another 1 minute.
5. Add beans, water, jalapenos, and beer to the pot. Bring mixture to a boil.
6. Transfer contents to a baking dish and place in middle of oven. Bake covered until beans are soft, about 1 1/2 to 2 hours. Stir in salt and bake an additional 10 minutes.
7. If beans begin to dry out during cooking, add more water so the mixture remains soupy with beans very soft but not falling apart.
8. Check seasoning and adjust salt if necessary.
9. Beans can be made up to 2 days ahead. Cool completely, cover, and refrigerate until ready to cook.
